S.FORM: mo mai pitu ne mi'o uhne 'oyonatmopsКү.
S.PHON: mo mai pitu ne miК”o uhne К”ojonatmopsЙЁ.
S.FORM: mo mai pitu ne mi'o uhne 'oyonatmopsКү.
S.PHON: mo mai pitu ne miК”o uhne К”ojonatmopsЙЁ.
S.TRANSL: I arrived at school at seven today.

S.FORM: ahoi tmopsКү, yКүmeКүmКү to 'oyomia tmopsКү 'o lema'cohio, mainca 'o letaayaezoi, "yac'Кү! m'ohkuyu!"
S.PHON: ahoi tmopsЙЁ, jЙЁmeЙЁmЙЁ to К”ojomia tmopsЙЁ К”o Й—emaК”КҰohio, mainКҰa К”o Й—etaajaezoi, "jaКҰК”ЙЁ! mК”ohkuju!"
S.FORM: ahoi tmopsКү, yКүmeКүmКү to 'oyomia tmopsКү 'o lema'cohio, mainca 'o letaayaezoi, "yac'Кү! m'ohkuyu!"
S.PHON: ahoi tmopsЙЁ, jЙЁmeЙЁmЙЁ to К”ojomia tmopsЙЁ К”o Й—emaК”КҰohio, mainКҰa К”o Й—etaajaezoi, "jaКҰК”ЙЁ! mК”ohkuju!"
S.TRANSL: It's time for class , the teacher walked into the classroom, and the monitor said: "Stand up! Salute!"

S.FORM: mimza tmunoi ho mainca, "'aveoveoyКү lema'cohio."
S.PHON: mimza tmunoi ho mainКҰa, "К”aveoveojЙЁ Й—emaК”КҰohio."
S.FORM: mimza tmunoi ho mainca, "'aveoveoyКү lema'cohio."
S.PHON: mimza tmunoi ho mainКҰa, "К”aveoveojЙЁ Й—emaК”КҰohio."

S.FORM: mo seolКүa no tmopsКү, isi pa'cohivi to lema'cohi tala hia tmasvКүtКү no buacou.
S.PHON: mo seoЙ—ЙЁa no tmopsЙЁ, isi paК”КҰohivi to Й—emaК”КҰohi taЙ—a hia tmasvЙЁtЙЁ no Й“uaКҰou.
S.FORM: mo seolКүa no tmopsКү, isi pa'cohivi to lema'cohi tala hia tmasvКүtКү no buacou.

S.TRANSL: In class, the teacher taught us the Cou language of numbers.

S.FORM: mimza tmu'ofehni to lema'cohio ho engha, mimza pasu tmasvКүtКү.
S.PHON: mimza tmuК”ofehni to Й—emaК”КҰohio ho eЕӢha, mimza pasu tmasvЙЁtЙЁ.
S.FORM: mimza tmu'ofehni to lema'cohio ho engha, mimza pasu tmasvКүtКү.
S.PHON: mimza tmuК”ofehni to Й—emaК”КҰohio ho eЕӢha, mimza pasu tmasvЙЁtЙЁ.
S.TRANSL: We followed the teacher to read, we sang the number song.

S.FORM: mo man'i 'o os'o pelКүi ci h'oe'ea cou.
S.PHON: mo manК”i К”o osК”o peЙ—ЙЁi КҰi hК”oeК”ea КҰou.
S.FORM: mo man'i 'o os'o pelКүi ci h'oe'ea cou.
S.PHON: mo manК”i К”o osК”o peЙ—ЙЁi КҰi hК”oeК”ea КҰou.
S.TRANSL: I have learned many Cou languages.

S.FORM: la'u huhucmasi uhne 'oyona tmopsКү, la'u huhucmasi aКүsvКүtКү buacou, te'ola esvКүtneni to ino'u.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u huhuКҰmasi uhne К”ojona tmopsЙЁ, Й—aК”u huhuКҰmasi aЙЁsvЙЁtЙЁ Й“uaКҰou, teК”oЙ—a esvЙЁtneni to inoК”u.
S.FORM: la'u huhucmasi uhne 'oyona tmopsКү, la'u huhucmasi aКүsvКүtКү buacou, te'ola esvКүtneni to ino'u.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u huhuКҰmasi uhne К”ojona tmopsЙЁ, Й—aК”u huhuКҰmasi aЙЁsvЙЁtЙЁ Й“uaКҰou, teК”oЙ—a esvЙЁtneni to inoК”u.
S.TRANSL: I go to school every day, I learn the Cou language every day, and I want to tell my mother.

S.FORM: mo cihi 'o ohaeva'u atai 'o ongkosi, mitacu tomaska veaucni.
S.PHON: mo КҰihi К”o ohaevaК”u atai К”o oЕӢkosi, mitaКҰu tomaska veauКҰni.

S.PHON: mo КҰihi К”o ohaevaК”u atai К”o oЕӢkosi, mitaКҰu tomaska veauКҰni.
S.TRANSL: I have a brother, his name is atai, he is 11 years old.

S.FORM: mimza yontomo conci 'oyonatmopsКү.
S.PHON: mimza jontomo КҰonКҰi К”ojonatmopsЙЁ.
S.FORM: mimza yontomo conci 'oyonatmopsКү.
S.PHON: mimza jontomo КҰonКҰi К”ojonatmopsЙЁ.
S.TRANSL: We study in the same school.

S.FORM: a'o ho ohaeva'u lamza na'no kaebКү pasunaeno.
S.PHON: aК”o ho ohaevaК”u Й—amza naК”no kaeЙ“ЙЁ pasunaeno.
S.FORM: a'o ho ohaeva'u lamza na'no kaebКү pasunaeno.
S.PHON: aК”o ho ohaevaК”u Й—amza naК”no kaeЙ“ЙЁ pasunaeno.
S.TRANSL: Both my brother and I like to sing.

S.FORM: la'u kaebКү yuhnguzu, homo seolКүa tosvo, lamza nКүtКү 'o mimza conoemoo ho yuhnguzu.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u kaeЙ“ЙЁ juhЕӢuzu, homo seoЙ—ЙЁa tosvo, Й—amza nЙЁtЙЁ К”o mimza КҰonoemoo ho juhЕӢuzu.
S.FORM: la'u kaebКү yuhnguzu, homo seolКүa tosvo, lamza nКүtКү 'o mimza conoemoo ho yuhnguzu.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u kaeЙ“ЙЁ juhЕӢuzu, homo seoЙ—ЙЁa tosvo, Й—amza nЙЁtЙЁ К”o mimza КҰonoemoo ho juhЕӢuzu.
S.TRANSL: I like swimming. During the holidays, our whole family go swimming together.

S.FORM: la'u kaebКү ho mi'o ea faeva ci nanghia, temiala melКү toehungu pasunaeno ho yuuhnguzu.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u kaeЙ“ЙЁ ho miК”o ea faeva КҰi naЕӢhia, temiaЙ—a meЙ—ЙЁ toehuЕӢu pasunaeno ho juuhЕӢuzu.
S.FORM: la'u kaebКү ho mi'o ea faeva ci nanghia, temiala melКү toehungu pasunaeno ho yuuhnguzu.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u kaeЙ“ЙЁ ho miК”o ea faeva КҰi naЕӢhia, temiaЙ—a meЙ—ЙЁ toehuЕӢu pasunaeno ho juuhЕӢuzu.
S.TRANSL: I love meeting new people, we can sing and swim together.

S.FORM: 'a oko no cou ao'.

S.FORM: 'a oko no cou ao'.
S.PHON: К”a oko no КҰou aoК”.

S.FORM: mo yone saviki 'o emoomza.

S.FORM: mo eimo 'o mimza conoemoo.
S.PHON: mo eimo К”o mimza КҰonoemoo.
S.TRANSL: There are five people in our family.

S.FORM: mo tuyu 'o mimza nanat'ot'ohaesa.
S.PHON: mo tuju К”o mimza nanatК”otК”ohaesa.
S.FORM: mo tuyu 'o mimza nanat'ot'ohaesa.
S.PHON: mo tuju К”o mimza nanatК”otК”ohaesa.
S.TRANSL: There are three brothers and sisters in our family.

S.FORM: a'o ho amo, ino, ohaeva no hahocngКү ho ohaeva no mamespingi lamza noteuyunu.
S.PHON: aК”o ho amo, ino, ohaeva no hahoКҰЕӢЙЁ ho ohaeva no mamespiЕӢi Й—amza noteujunu.
S.FORM: a'o ho amo, ino, ohaeva no hahocngКү ho ohaeva no mamespingi lamza noteuyunu.
S.PHON: aК”o ho amo, ino, ohaeva no hahoКҰЕӢЙЁ ho ohaeva no mamespiЕӢi Й—amza noteujunu.
S.TRANSL: I live with my father, mother, brother and sister.

S.FORM: mo bankake 'o amo'u, zou lesmo'eisi 'o amo'u.
S.PHON: mo Й“ankake К”o amoК”u, zou Й—esmoК”eisi К”o amoК”u.
S.FORM: mo bankake 'o amo'u, zou lesmo'eisi 'o amo'u.
S.PHON: mo Й“ankake К”o amoК”u, zou Й—esmoК”eisi К”o amoК”u.
S.TRANSL: My father is tall and he is a policeman.

S.FORM: mo kКүehoi 'o ino'u, zou leaotothomКү 'o ino'u.
S.PHON: mo kЙЁehoi К”o inoК”u, zou Й—eaotothomЙЁ К”o inoК”u.
S.FORM: mo kКүehoi 'o ino'u, zou leaotothomКү 'o ino'u.
S.PHON: mo kЙЁehoi К”o inoК”u, zou Й—eaotothomЙЁ К”o inoК”u.
S.TRANSL: My mother is thin and my mom is a doctor.

S.FORM: 'o amo hola yontoemoo lasi uaneni buacou a'mi.
S.PHON: К”o amo hoЙ—a jontoemoo Й—asi uaneni Й“uaКҰou aК”mi.
S.FORM: 'o amo hola yontoemoo lasi uaneni buacou a'mi.
S.PHON: К”o amo hoЙ—a jontoemoo Й—asi uaneni Й“uaКҰou aК”mi.
S.TRANSL: My father speaks the ethnic language with us at home.

S.FORM: ho lamza tosvo lamza acКүhКү maine'e ne huehungu.
S.PHON: ho Й—amza tosvo Й—amza aКҰЙЁhЙЁ maineК”e ne huehuЕӢu.
S.FORM: ho lamza tosvo lamza acКүhКү maine'e ne huehungu.
S.PHON: ho Й—amza tosvo Й—amza aКҰЙЁhЙЁ maineК”e ne huehuЕӢu.
S.TRANSL: We will all go back to the tribe during the summer vacation.

S.FORM: a'o ho ma'ahaeva lamza tКүtpКүtКү to kio.
S.PHON: aК”o ho maК”ahaeva Й—amza tЙЁtpЙЁtЙЁ to kio.
S.FORM: a'o ho ma'ahaeva lamza tКүtpКүtКү to kio.
S.PHON: aК”o ho maК”ahaeva Й—amza tЙЁtpЙЁtЙЁ to kio.
S.TRANSL: My brothers and sisters and I will catch frogs together.

S.FORM: la'u na'na КүmnКүa 'o cou to emoo'u.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u naК”na ЙЁmnЙЁa К”o КҰou to emooК”u.
S.FORM: la'u na'na КүmnКүa 'o cou to emoo'u.
S.PHON: Й—aК”u naК”na ЙЁmnЙЁa К”o КҰou to emooК”u.
S.TRANSL: I like our family very much.

S.FORM: mimza yuyuso 'e ino ho covnКү maitan'e.
S.PHON: mimza jujuso К”e ino ho КҰovnЙЁ maitanК”e.
S.FORM: mimza yuyuso 'e ino ho covnКү maitan'e.
S.PHON: mimza jujuso К”e ino ho КҰovnЙЁ maitanК”e.
S.TRANSL: I went out with my mother today.

S.FORM: na'no tatposa 'e pepe homo hie, o'amo soyКүmКү o'amo mu'ei, na'no sohuyu 'e poepe.
S.PHON: naК”no tatposa К”e pepe homo hie, oК”amo sojЙЁmЙЁ oК”amo muК”ei, naК”no sohuju К”e poepe.
S.FORM: na'no tatposa 'e pepe homo hie, o'amo soyКүmКү o'amo mu'ei, na'no sohuyu 'e poepe.
S.PHON: naК”no tatposa К”e pepe homo hie, oК”amo sojЙЁmЙЁ oК”amo muК”ei, naК”no sohuju К”e poepe.
S.TRANSL: The sky is blue during the day, the weather is neither cold nor hot, and the wind is cool.

S.FORM: mimza yootamo yaakukuzo ci ceoaho yaa bobonКү, sliКүa ta hia co feango, na'no noyano.
S.PHON: mimza jootamo jaakukuzo КҰi КҰeoaho jaa Й“oЙ“onЙЁ, sЙ—iЙЁa ta hia КҰo feaЕӢo, naК”no nojano.
S.FORM: mimza yootamo yaakukuzo ci ceoaho yaa bobonКү, sliКүa ta hia co feango, na'no noyano.
S.PHON: mimza jootamo jaakukuzo КҰi КҰeoaho jaa Й“oЙ“onЙЁ, sЙ—iЙЁa ta hia КҰo feaЕӢo, naК”no nojano.
S.TRANSL: We ate bread on the grass, the sun was shining on us, and it was very warm.

S.FORM: ataveisi no cohzona, smoefКүhngya 'epepe.
S.PHON: ataveisi no КҰohzona, smoefЙЁhЕӢja К”epepe.
S.FORM: ataveisi no cohzona, smoefКүhngya 'epepe.
S.PHON: ataveisi no КҰohzona, smoefЙЁhЕӢja К”epepe.
S.TRANSL: In the afternoon, the sky turned red.

S.FORM: os'o tuocosi 'e ino, mancis'a smoefКүhngoya 'epepe.
S.PHON: osК”o tuoКҰosi К”e ino, manКҰisК”a smoefЙЁhЕӢoja К”epepe.
S.FORM: os'o tuocosi 'e ino, mancis'a smoefКүhngoya 'epepe.
S.PHON: osК”o tuoКҰosi К”e ino, manКҰisК”a smoefЙЁhЕӢoja К”epepe.
S.TRANSL: I asked my mother why the sky is red.

S.FORM: yainca ta ino, teasonКү cmu'ho na cmoehu.
S.PHON: jainКҰa ta ino, teasonЙЁ КҰmuК”ho na КҰmoehu.
S.FORM: yainca ta ino, teasonКү cmu'ho na cmoehu.
S.PHON: jainКҰa ta ino, teasonЙЁ КҰmuК”ho na КҰmoehu.
S.TRANSL: Mom said, maybe the typhoon is coming.

S.FORM: uk'a ci meipeipi ta engКүca homo yofna, micu yuyafo si congeoha.
S.PHON: ukК”a КҰi meipeipi ta eЕӢЙЁКҰa homo jofna, miКҰu jujafo si КҰoЕӢeoha.
S.FORM: uk'a ci meipeipi ta engКүca homo yofna, micu yuyafo si congeoha.
S.PHON: ukК”a КҰi meipeipi ta eЕӢЙЁКҰa homo jofna, miКҰu jujafo si КҰoЕӢeoha.
S.TRANSL: There were no clouds in the sky at night, and the stars came out.

S.FORM: tetocu maezocu maine'e.
S.PHON: tetoКҰu maezoКҰu maineК”e.
S.FORM: tetocu maezocu maine'e.
S.PHON: tetoКҰu maezoКҰu maineК”e.

S.FORM: smaimio ta ceono maine'e, mi'o mi'unu to feohКү m'ea tooyokeo.
S.PHON: smaimio ta КҰeono maineК”e, miК”o miК”unu to feohЙЁ mК”ea toojokeo.
S.FORM: smaimio ta ceono maine'e, mi'o mi'unu to feohКү m'ea tooyokeo.
S.PHON: smaimio ta КҰeono maineК”e, miК”o miК”unu to feohЙЁ mК”ea toojokeo.
S.TRANSL: On the way home, I made a wish with the stars,

S.FORM: atva'esi КүmnКү ote esmi na cmoehu, mi'on'a mici mimiyo.
S.PHON: atvaК”esi ЙЁmnЙЁ ote esmi na КҰmoehu, miК”onК”a miКҰi mimijo.
S.FORM: atva'esi КүmnКү ote esmi na cmoehu, mi'on'a mici mimiyo.
S.PHON: atvaК”esi ЙЁmnЙЁ ote esmi na КҰmoehu, miК”onК”a miКҰi mimijo.
S.TRANSL: I hope the typhoon doesn't come, I still want to go out to play again.

S.FORM: 'o la'u yoni ci 'oyonatmopsКү mo nocmu'u to emoo'mia, la'u huhucmasi coeconКү ho uhto 'oyonatmopsКү, maezo la'u coeconКү ho main'e.

S.FORM: 'o la'u yoni ci 'oyonatmopsКү mo nocmu'u to emoo'mia, la'u huhucmasi coeconКү ho uhto 'oyonatmopsКү, maezo la'u coeconКү ho main'e.
S.PHON: К”o Й—aК”u joni КҰi К”ojonatmopsЙЁ mo noКҰmuК”u to emooК”mia, Й—aК”u huhuКҰmasi КҰoeКҰonЙЁ ho uhto К”ojonatmopsЙЁ, maezo Й—aК”u КҰoeКҰonЙЁ ho mainК”e.
S.TRANSL: My school is next to our house, I walk to school every day, and I also walk home.

S.FORM: o'a mo meoisi 'o lamza yoni ci 'oyonatmopsКү, o'amo botngonКү 'o 'o'okono tmopsКү, lamza acКүhКү yupa bobohngКү.
S.PHON: oК”a mo meoisi К”o Й—amza joni КҰi К”ojonatmopsЙЁ, oК”amo Й“otЕӢonЙЁ К”o К”oК”okono tmopsЙЁ, Й—amza aКҰЙЁhЙЁ jupa Й“oЙ“ohЕӢЙЁ.
S.FORM: o'a mo meoisi 'o lamza yoni ci 'oyonatmopsКү, o'amo botngonКү 'o 'o'okono tmopsКү, lamza acКүhКү yupa bobohngКү.
S.PHON: oК”a mo meoisi К”o Й—amza joni КҰi К”ojonatmopsЙЁ, oК”amo Й“otЕӢonЙЁ К”o К”oК”okono tmopsЙЁ, Й—amza aКҰЙЁhЙЁ jupa Й“oЙ“ohЕӢЙЁ.
S.TRANSL: Our school is not big, there are not many students, everyone knows each other.

S.FORM: la acКүhКү yu'eteyunu to 'oyonayoyaso 'o 'o'oko, toehungu peispak'i to ma'li, noteuyunu ho aumomotКү'Кү.

S.FORM: la acКүhКү yu'eteyunu to 'oyonayoyaso 'o 'o'oko, toehungu peispak'i to ma'li, noteuyunu ho aumomotКү'Кү.
S.PHON: Й—a aКҰЙЁhЙЁ juК”etejunu to К”ojonajojaso К”o К”oК”oko, toehuЕӢu peispakК”i to maК”Й—i, noteujunu ho aumomotЙЁК”ЙЁ.
S.TRANSL: All the children will gather in the playground, play ball and chat together.

S.FORM: la maezo tes'upu 'amia 'o lema'cohio ho peispak'i, zou cono emoo 'e 'oyonatmopsКү.
S.PHON: Й—a maezo tesК”upu К”amia К”o Й—emaК”КҰohio ho peispakК”i, zou КҰono emoo К”e К”ojonatmopsЙЁ.
S.FORM: la maezo tes'upu 'amia 'o lema'cohio ho peispak'i, zou cono emoo 'e 'oyonatmopsКү.
S.PHON: Й—a maezo tesК”upu К”amia К”o Й—emaК”КҰohio ho peispakК”i, zou КҰono emoo К”e К”ojonatmopsЙЁ.
S.TRANSL: The teachers also play with us, the school is one big family.

S.FORM: lamza huhucmasi ateoteop'КүcКү, la acКүha tusi'nga ho poa cofkoya 'ola 'oyonavenkio.

S.TRANSL: When we were young, our family lived in tfuya'o. After I started school, we moved to Chiayi and have been living in the city ever since.

S.FORM: mo'ucula tmopsКү to taingakoo, mi'o tmopsКү ho aКүsvКүtКү to ceoceona no h'oyunsova ta cou, peela cohivi mo a'КүmtКү mav'ov'o 'o h'oyunsova ta cou ta taivang.
S.PHON: moК”uКҰuЙ—a tmopsЙЁ to taiЕӢakoo, miК”o tmopsЙЁ ho aЙЁsvЙЁtЙЁ to КҰeoКҰeona no hК”ojunsova ta КҰou, peeЙ—a КҰohivi mo aК”ЙЁmtЙЁ mavК”ovК”o К”o hК”ojunsova ta КҰou ta taivaЕӢ.

S.PHON: moК”uКҰuЙ—a tmopsЙЁ to taiЕӢakoo, miК”o tmopsЙЁ ho aЙЁsvЙЁtЙЁ to КҰeoКҰeona no hК”ojunsova ta КҰou, peeЙ—a КҰohivi mo aК”ЙЁmtЙЁ mavК”ovК”o К”o hК”ojunsova ta КҰou ta taivaЕӢ.
S.TRANSL: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

S.TRANSL: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

S.TRANSL: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

S.TRANSL: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in cou, as well as Japanese, Chinese and Bunun.

S.TRANSL: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunun, and he knows many Chinese at work, so he can speak so many languages.

S.TRANSL: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

S.TRANSL: When I was young, I didn't understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

S.PHON: jainКҰa to pasuja, "na aЙЁmtЙЁ oЕӢkoto К”aeno К”e isi uaneniКҰou ho tposi КҰi oЕӢko na aЙЁmtЙЁ oЕӢkoto. К”e oЕӢkoК”u zou oЕӢko to К”oahЕӢЙЁК”u, zou oЕӢko to mimia КҰonoemoo, zou oЕӢko mia, tataК”ia te asuhКҰu Й“otЕӢonЙЁ nate peЙ—a eК”Й—ЙЁa na iaКҰhi oЕӢko."
S.TRANSL: pasuya said, \The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group

S.FORM: ne hucma mimia yuyuso 'e ba'i ho uhtola toehunga hoteyuni mhinmhino te soovai, 'o ba'i mo mahafo to isi iachia emКү'a ci machachae te soovai, maezo moyai to mafe cila ana to cou ci 'oanКү ho faeni tomo batngonКү.
S.PHON: ne huКҰma mimia jujuso К”e Й“aК”i ho uhtoЙ—a toehuЕӢa hotejuni mhinmhino te soovai, К”o Й“aК”i mo mahafo to isi iaКҰhia emЙЁК”a КҰi maКҰhaКҰhae te soovai, maezo mojai to mafe КҰiЙ—a ana to КҰou КҰi К”oanЙЁ ho faeni tomo Й“atЕӢonЙЁ.
S.FORM: ne hucma mimia yuyuso 'e ba'i ho uhtola toehunga hoteyuni mhinmhino te soovai, 'o ba'i mo mahafo to isi iachia emКү'a ci machachae te soovai, maezo moyai to mafe cila ana to cou ci 'oanКү ho faeni tomo batngonКү.
S.PHON: ne huКҰma mimia jujuso К”e Й“aК”i ho uhtoЙ—a toehuЕӢa hotejuni mhinmhino te soovai, К”o Й“aК”i mo mahafo to isi iaКҰhia emЙЁК”a КҰi maКҰhaКҰhae te soovai, maezo mojai to mafe КҰiЙ—a ana to КҰou КҰi К”oanЙЁ ho faeni tomo Й“atЕӢonЙЁ.
S.TRANSL: Yesterday, I went to the market with my grandmother to set up a stall. My grandmother brought a lot of vegetables that she grew herself and also prepared indigenous cuisine for everyone.

S.FORM: ihe na'na КүmnКүa 'o soovai to ba'i, 'o cuc'u ho tomato mo botngonКү 'omo mhino, botngonКү 'omo uso ho oothomКү to isi pei'i ci chae.
S.PHON: ihe naК”na ЙЁmnЙЁa К”o soovai to Й“aК”i, К”o КҰuКҰК”u ho tomato mo Й“otЕӢonЙЁ К”omo mhino, Й“otЕӢonЙЁ К”omo uso ho oothomЙЁ to isi peiК”i КҰi КҰhae.
S.FORM: ihe na'na КүmnКүa 'o soovai to ba'i, 'o cuc'u ho tomato mo botngonКү 'omo mhino, botngonКү 'omo uso ho oothomКү to isi pei'i ci chae.
S.PHON: ihe naК”na ЙЁmnЙЁa К”o soovai to Й“aК”i, К”o КҰuКҰК”u ho tomato mo Й“otЕӢonЙЁ К”omo mhino, Й“otЕӢonЙЁ К”omo uso ho oothomЙЁ to isi peiК”i КҰi КҰhae.
S.TRANSL: Everyone loved my grandmother's stall. Many people bought the chilis and African eggplants, and many people came to taste the dishes she cooked.

S.FORM: 'oisi pei'i ci chae hКүngКү ho ufi na'no mafe, ho i'o isi titheni to fou fuzu ho toeteuyuni pei'i 'o ma'fКү ci fou, 'o na'ausnasi ihe na'no kaeba tomo botngonКү ci cou, acКүhКү uhto soovaimia ho bitoyange.
S.PHON: К”oisi peiК”i КҰi КҰhae hЙЁЕӢЙЁ ho ufi naК”no mafe, ho iК”o isi titheni to fou fuzu ho toeteujuni peiК”i К”o maК”fЙЁ КҰi fou, К”o naК”ausnasi ihe naК”no kaeЙ“a tomo Й“otЕӢonЙЁ КҰi КҰou, aКҰЙЁhЙЁ uhto soovaimia ho Й“itojaЕӢe.

S.TRANSL: The ferns and sticky rice she cooked were delicious, as well as the braised pork with maqaw pepper and wild boar meat. The flavors attracted many customers, and everyone lined up at our stall.

S.FORM: nemo seolКүa no apapeipei'i 'o ba'i, mi'o yonto ciengonasi ho meukokosКү tomo sucocafi ci cou. mi'o yonto ciengonasi tmunoi moozoyКү, "'e chae c'o acКүha iachia emКү'a, acКүhКү yКү'Кү'Күmni ci chae, aupcio mhino!

S.TRANSL: all-natural and organic! Come and buy now!\""
While my grandmother was cooking, I was on the side attracting customers. I shouted, \The vegetables are self-grown

S.FORM: 'o macucumamia c'o osnia psКүepКүngneni, yainca to ba'i, "moc'o acКүhКү i'mi ho miso to'usnu."
S.PHON: К”o maКҰuКҰumamia КҰК”o osnia psЙЁepЙЁЕӢneni, jainКҰa to Й“aК”i, "moКҰК”o aКҰЙЁhЙЁ iК”mi ho miso toК”usnu."
S.FORM: 'o macucumamia c'o osnia psКүepКүngneni, yainca to ba'i, "moc'o acКүhКү i'mi ho miso to'usnu."

S.TRANSL: Our goods sold out quickly, and my grandmother said, "It's all thanks to your help."

S.FORM: 'emo maica ci hi'hioa, mi'o tov'ohКү ta nat'Күca no cacКүfea ta cou ho mau'to'tohКүngКү no ma'o'oanКү, avinano peela cohivi na ngoseo tala soovai.

S.TRANSL: Through this event, I learned about indigenous plants and food and experienced the hard work of running a stall.
